title: Zazen (Zen)
blurb: Just sitting — the heart of Zen practice.
lead: Zazen is the seated meditation at the core of Zen. Its spirit is 'just sitting' — being fully present without chasing any goal.

blocks.text: Zazen means 'seated meditation'. In the Soto Zen tradition it is practised as shikantaza — 'just sitting' — resting in open, alert presence without striving for any particular experience.
It is a spiritual discipline. Its value is found through steady practice within the tradition rather than measured by research.

items: Sit on a cushion or chair with a stable, upright posture; hands resting in your lap.
Keep the eyes half-open, gaze lowered a few feet ahead.
Rather than focusing on one object, allow everything to be as it is — thoughts come and go like clouds.
When you notice you've drifted into thinking, gently return to open, upright presence.

blocks.text: Straining to 'empty the mind'. Zazen isn't about forcing blankness — it's about not clinging to what passes through.

resources.title: Introduction to Zen meditation
resources.whyHere: Free, clear zazen instruction from an established Zen center.
